attacking_style: Not a traditional attacker; excels in reduced bunch sprints after selective climbs (2-5km, 3-6% gradients). Launches early and sustained (150-250m out), relying on power over positioning.
team_tactics: Bahrain frequently mismanages leadout—multiple observations of poor positioning, premature efforts, and breakaway control failures despite team commitment. Often isolated in final 500m, forcing long solo sprints.
terrain_preference: Punchy uphill finishes (3-6%) after selective climbs that shed pure sprinters but retain rouleurs. Paris-Roubaix wet cobbles revealed exceptional resilience. Struggles on sustained climbs >8km and technical flat sprints requiring pure positioning.
vulnerability: Short steep climbs (6-8km at 5%+) drop him unexpectedly. Poor sprint timing—launches too early (200m+) repeatedly, fading final 50m. Team tactical failures leave him isolated or chasing lost breakaways. Health fragility post-2021 (bronchitis, medical intervention).
key_patterns: Consistently fastest finisher in reduced sprints, but converts poorly due to positioning/timing errors (7 instances)
Launches sprints exceptionally early (150-250m), relying on sustained power rather than explosive acceleration (5 occurrences)
Team leadout failures force isolated efforts; rarely has proper train despite being protected rider (6+ races)
Thrives when climbs shed pure sprinters but struggles when gradients exceed 5-6% sustained (4 observations)
Health concerns post-2021: bronchitis complications and medical intervention suggest compromised recovery capacity (2 documented incidents)
Tactically hesitant on opponent's wheels despite superior raw speed, costing wins against Sagan-level riders (3 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: Peak 2021 form (Roubaix monument, Dauphiné dominance) collapsed in 2022 with health issues (bronchitis, post-race medical intervention). Dropped unexpectedly on moderate climbs suggesting systemic decline.
